calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Sep-09-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER DILYS BART, M.D.'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motion for attorney's fees is denied.

Plaintiff fails to demonstrate that respondent's defense of this action was "frivolous, unreasonable, without foundation, or in bad faith" within the meaning of Business & Professions Code sec. 809.9. (See Economy v. Sutter East Bay Hospitals (2019) 31 Cal.App.5th 1147, 1165 [defining standard to recover fees under section 809.9].)

While it is true that respondent's service of the notice of adverse action did not comply with its bylaws, a reasonable attorney could adopt respondent's position and petitioner fails to demonstrate bad faith. (Mir v. Charter Suburban Hospital (1994) 27 Cal.App.4th 1471 [merely overturning hospital's peer review decision alone is insufficient for fees].) Respondent's FedEx service of the notice was not dramatically different from the sanctioned modes of service set forth in the bylaws. A reasonable attorney could conclude that there was substantial compliance and/or petitioner waived the issue by accepting FedEx service for other communications. (Declaration of Clark at 17; Judicial Council of California v. Jacobs Facilities, Inc. (2015) 239 Cal.App.4th 882, 914 [explaining equitable doctrine of substantial compliance].) Respondent had information from FedEx that the notice was served at petitioner's household and reasonably inferred that she had actual notice. (Declaration of Clark at 12 and Ex. D; Declaration of Malone at 17.) In sum, petitioner fails to maintain its evidentiary burden under section 809.9.

Order signed.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/CK)

Notice Of Hearing On First Amended Verified Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandate
Off Calendar Jul-29-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER DILYS BART, M.D.'S First Amended Verified Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandate.

The parties' verification arguments are rejected. "The object of a verification is to assure good faith in the averments or statements of a party." Frio v. Sup. Crt. (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1480, 1498. There is no questions but that the parties operated in good faith. Further, on May 27, 2022, the court determined that the amended petition was properly verified. CRC 2.257(b)(2) [electronic signature]; 5/20/22 Bart Decl., 2, Exhibit 1.

Respondent was obliged to give petitioner written notice of its adverse action. Business & Professions Code sec. 809.1(b) ["The peer review body shall give the licentiate written notice of the final proposed action."]. Respondent was obliged to comply with the notice provisions in its Bylaws. Business & Professions Code sec. 809.6(a) ["The parties are bound by any additional notice and hearing provisions contained in any applicable professional society or medical staff bylaws"]. The Petition properly seeks writ relief based on respondent's failure to comply with its Bylaws and the law. CCP sec. 1085(a) ["A writ of mandate may be issued by any court to any inferior tribunal, corporation, board, or person, to compel the performance of an act which the law specially enjoins"

Section 9.2(c) of respondent's Bylaws provides that service of the Notice of Adverse Action "shall be sent by hand or by certified or registered mail." 3/23/22 Weinberg Decl., Ex. 1. Here, the record unequivocally demonstrates that respondent failed to comply with this notice provision. Respondent sent the Notice by FedEx and petitioner did not receive the Notice. Verified Amended Petition, 11; 7/18/22 Clark Decl., 12. Respondent thus failed to comply with its Bylaws. Respondent fails to demonstrate that petitioner waived her right to a hearing or that laches bars this action.

The court orders respondent to provide a hearing to petitioner in compliance with sec. 9.2 - 9.4 of the Bylaws. The court further orders respondent to notify the Medical Board by August 19, 2022, that the court has vacated its notice of adverse action since petitioner is still litigating the propriety of the denial of her application.

Order is signed.

Judge: Curtis E Karnow, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/CEK)

Notice Of Hearing On First Amended Verified Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandate (Notices)
Off Calendar May-27-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER DILYS BART, M.D.'S First Amended Verified Petition For Peremptory Writ Of Administrative Mandate.

Petitioner's petition for writ of mandate is denied without prejudice.

The evidence indicates that the amended petition is properly verified. (See CRC 2.257(b)(2); Declaration of Dilys Bart, 2, Exhibit 1.)

Respondent fails to show that the petition is untimely or that laches applies. Respondent's complaint that petitioner is improperly seeking a peremptory writ is baseless since the court instructed petitioner to do so by noticed motion when it denied petitioner's ex parte alternative writ request.

Respondent correctly notes that petitioner filed and served this motion before respondent's time to respond had run. (See Declaration of Malone, at 21; CCP 1089.5.) Therefore, the motion for writ of mandate is denied without prejudice. Petitioner may re-set this matter once respondent answers the amended petition.

Order is signed.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/SRB)
